questions: Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been handling your mother's medical appointments and paperwork for the past seven months — her doctor's office just emailed asking you to fill out another round of insurance forms. How do you approach it?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. Your team has had the same recurring bug in the codebase for three months — it keeps getting patched and reappearing in new places. The QA team just flagged it again this morning. What do you do?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been the primary caregiver for two young kids through a difficult stretch and tonight is the fourth consecutive evening where dinner, baths, and bedtime all fall to you. How do you get through the evening?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been managing a slow, complicated home renovation for eight months — the contractor just called to say there's another delay and they need to revisit the timeline again. How do you handle the call?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been covering for an absent colleague for six weeks on top of your own workload — your manager sends a message this morning saying it'll be at least two more weeks. What's your response?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. A dispute with your landlord over a repair has been going back and forth for four months with no resolution — they email again today with another excuse. How do you reply?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been monitoring a chronic health issue with weekly check-ins, adjustments, and follow-ups for over a year — your doctor calls to say they want to try yet another treatment approach. How do you take that?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. Your organization has been going through a restructuring for nine months — another all-hands meeting just got added to the calendar to discuss further changes. How do you prepare for it?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been in the middle of a difficult friendship conflict for weeks, mediating between two people who keep pulling you back in — one of them messages again tonight wanting to process. How do you respond?

Give an energetic, enthusiastic response. You've been job searching for five months with a mix of near-misses and rejections — another promising application just came back as a no. You have two more applications in progress. How do you think about what to do next?
